title: 10 best herbal tinctures for jet lag
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for jet lag are concentrated plant extracts designed to alleviate the discomforts associated with travel across time zones. These tinctures help regulate the body's internal clock, reducing symptoms such as fatigue, insomnia, and disorientation. Examples of effective herbal tinctures include Valerian root, which promotes relaxation and improves sleep quality, and Ginkgo biloba, which enhances cognitive function and reduces jet lag-related brain fog. By incorporating these tinctures into their routine, travelers can improve the quality of their rest, increase energy levels, and enjoy a more seamless transition to their new destination.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tinctures helps with jet lag because its sedative properties promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, making it easier to adjust to a new sleep schedule. As valerian interacts with GABA receptors in the brain, it can help regulate the body's internal clock, allowing travelers to fall asleep faster and sleep more soundly. Additionally, valerian tinctures can increase the quality of sleep, reducing grogginess and fatigue associated with jet lag. This natural remedy helps alleviate the discomfort of traveling across time zones, making it easier to feel rested and refreshed upon arrival at your destination.
remedy_properties: Sedative properties: Valerian root has a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help regulate sleep patterns and reduce stress caused by jet lag.
Adaptogenic properties: Valerian's adaptogenic properties allow it to help the body adjust to changes in environment and routine, making it easier to adapt to new time zones and rhythms.
Anxiolytic properties: Valerian has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can reduce anxiety and restlessness caused by jet lag, promoting relaxation and improved sleep.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary source of valerenic acid, a key compound responsible for promoting relaxation and reducing stress, making them ideal for addressing symptoms of jet lag.

Rhyzomes: Valerian rhyzomes contain a unique combination of terpenes and flavonoids that help to regulate sleep-wake cycles and reduce anxiety, making them effective in treating jet lag-related insomnia.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried valeriana officinalis roots at dawn or before noon for optimal potency.
Chop the harvested root material into small pieces and soak it in high-proof ethanol or vodka for 4-6 weeks.
Monitor the tincture's color and consistency, shaking the mixture daily to ensure efficient extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids after 2-3 hours.
Store the finished tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years, taking 20-40 drops 3 times daily as needed for jet lag relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with jet lag because its calming and sedative properties can help regulate your body's internal clock. The herb contains rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to improve sleep quality by reducing stress and anxiety. By promoting relaxation and calming the mind and body, lemon balm tincture can help you adjust to a new time zone faster and get a good night's sleep, even when traveling across multiple time zones. This can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag, such as fatigue, insomnia, and disorientation.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Lemon balm's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation caused by long-distance travel, promoting a smoother transition to local time zones.

Calming and Sedative Effects: The calming and sedative properties of lemon balm tincture can help regulate sleep patterns and promote relaxation, making it easier to fall asleep at the new destination's bedtime.

Digestive Soothing Properties: Lemon balm's carminative and anti-spasmodic compounds can alleviate digestive discomforts associated with air travel, such as bloating, cramps, and nausea, allowing for a more comfortable journey.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in calcium and magnesium, which help regulate sleep patterns and reduce anxiety associated with jet lag.
Flowers: The flowers contain flavonoids that have a calming effect on the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing stress caused by time zone changes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh melissa officinalis leaves on a sunny day in mid-summer for optimal potency.
Clean and chop 2 cups of the freshly harvested leaves into small pieces to facilitate drying.
Dry the chopped leaves using a food dehydrator or air-drying method at 95°f for 24 hours to preserve nutrients.
Combine 1 cup of dried melissa officinalis leaves with 2 cups of high-proof vodka in a glass jar and seal tightly, steeping for 14 days without light exposure.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth into another glass container, discarding solids, and store the final product in dark amber glass bottles at room temperature for up to 3 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tinctures helps with jet lag because of its unique properties that regulate the body's internal clock. The tincture contains a compound called passiflora incarnata, which has been shown to promote relaxation and reduce stress levels, making it easier for the body to adjust to new time zones. Additionally, maypop's adaptogenic properties help the body adapt to changes in its natural rhythms, reducing symptoms of jet lag such as fatigue, insomnia, and disorientation.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic effects: Maypop tincture helps your body adapt to new environments and rhythms, reducing stress and anxiety caused by travel-induced disruptions.

Sedative and calming properties: The flavonoids and alkaloids in Maypop tincture promote relaxation and calmness, making it easier to fall asleep despite the body's natural resistance to sleep after crossing time zones.
Antioxidant activity: Maypop tincture's antioxidant properties help mitigate oxidative stress caused by jet lag, which can lead to fatigue, irritability, and other symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Maypop plant have been traditionally used to create calming and soothing tinctures, which can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as anxiety and insomnia.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the Maypop plant contain sedative compounds that can promote relaxation and reduce stress, making them an effective addition to tinctures for jet lag relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh passiflora incarnata leaves and flowers at dawn.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces using scissors or a mortar and pestle.
Combine the chopped herbs with 500ml of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with jet lag because they contain a unique blend of adaptogenic compounds that help regulate the body's natural circadian rhythms. The tincture's gentle, balancing effects on the nervous system and hormonal fluctuations can alleviate symptoms of fatigue, insomnia, and disorientation commonly experienced during time changes. Additionally, maidenhair tree's calming properties can reduce stress and anxiety associated with travel, allowing for a smoother transition back to local time. This natural remedy can help you adjust to your new environment and get a good night's sleep in no time.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals in the body, which can contribute to fatigue and disorientation caused by jet lag.

Cerebral vasodilator effects: Ginkgo biloba's ability to dilate blood vessels in the brain can help improve circulation and reduce inflammation, which may alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as headaches and cognitive fogginess.

Adaptogenic properties: Ginkgo biloba is known for its adaptogenic effects, meaning it helps the body adapt to stress and change, which can be particularly helpful when traveling across time zones and adjusting to a new environment.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and adaptogenic properties, making them effective in reducing fatigue and promoting relaxation.
Seeds: High in melatonin and serotonin, helping regulate sleep patterns and alleviate symptoms of jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves in autumn or spring for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm dark place for 7-10 days or until crispy.
Chop the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into small pieces and combine with 80 proof vodka at a ratio of 1:5.
Store the mixture in an amber glass jar for 6-8 weeks, shaking daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve and bottle for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oats tinctures helps with jet lag because they contain soothing compounds like avenanthramides, which can calm the nervous system and promote relaxation. This allows your body to adjust to its new time zone more easily, reducing symptoms of fatigue, insomnia, and disorientation that often accompany jet lag. Additionally, oat tinctures have been shown to regulate cortisol levels, helping to stabilize circadian rhythms and facilitate a smoother transition into local time. By promoting deep relaxation and regulating bodily functions, oat tinctures can help mitigate the effects of jet lag and get you feeling refreshed and revitalized in no time.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic Properties: Oat tincture helps the body adapt to new time zones by regulating cortisol levels and promoting a sense of balance, reducing stress and anxiety caused by jet lag.
Sedative and Relaxing Effects: The tincture's calming properties can help induce relaxation and reduce insomnia, making it easier to fall asleep at a new bedtime and adjust to the local time zone.

Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Activity: Oat tincture's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties can help mitigate the negative effects of jet lag on the body, such as fatigue, headaches, and digestive issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help regulate sleep-wake cycles and alleviate symptoms of jet lag.
Barks: Contains flavonoids that have been shown to improve sleep quality and reduce stress caused by travel disruptions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest avena sativa oat tops and stems at peak ripeness for optimal potency.
Clean and dry the harvested material using a food dehydrator on low heat.
Chop the dried oats into small pieces and combine with 80 proof vodka in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks to allow for extraction and infusion.
Strain and filter the tincture using cheesecloth or coffee filters and transfer to dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tinctures helps with jet lag because it calms the mind and body, reducing symptoms of fatigue, insomnia, and anxiety often associated with time zone changes. The tranquil properties of lavender tincture work to regulate sleep patterns and promote a restful night's sleep, allowing travelers to adjust quickly to their new environment. Additionally, lavender's adaptogenic effects help to normalize the body's natural rhythms, ensuring a smoother transition to the local time zone.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic properties: Lavender's anxiolytic effects can help reduce stress and anxiety caused by travel disruptions, allowing for a smoother transition to a new time zone.
Sedative properties: The sedative compounds in lavender tincture can promote relaxation and improve sleep quality, helping to counteract the effects of jet lag on your body's internal clock.

Adaptogenic properties: Lavender's adaptogenic effects enable it to help regulate the body's response to stress and adapt to new situations, which is particularly useful when traveling across time zones and experiencing disruptions in daily routine.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lavender roots are valued for their calming and relaxing properties, making them an excellent choice for alleviating symptoms of jet lag such as insomnia and anxiety.
Buds: Lavender buds contain a high concentration of essential oils, which help regulate the body's natural rhythms and promote relaxation during travel-related disruptions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried lavender flowers at dawn or early morning for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested lavender flowers in a warm well-ventilated area for several hours or use a food dehydrator.
Chop the dried lavender flowers into small pieces to increase surface area and facilitate extraction.
Combine 1 part chopped lavender with 5 parts high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar, leaving about an inch of headspace.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar every few days to aid in extraction.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St john's wort tinctures helps with jet lag because of its unique ability to regulate the body's internal clock. As a natural adaptogen, it helps the body adjust to new time zones by promoting a sense of relaxation and reducing stress levels. By calming the mind and body, St john's wort tincture allows individuals to easily transition into their new surroundings, minimizing the effects of jet lag and ensuring a smoother travel experience.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: St. John's Wort helps the body adapt to stress, including the physical and mental fatigue caused by jet lag, allowing you to adjust more easily to new time zones.

Melatonin regulation: St. John's Wort has been shown to regulate melatonin levels in the body, which can help alleviate symptoms of insomnia, anxiety, and other sleep disturbances commonly experienced during jet lag.

Mood-enhancing effects: St. John's Wort is known for its antidepressant properties, which can help reduce feelings of irritability, anxiety, and stress that often accompany jet lag, promoting a more relaxed and balanced state.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain bioflavonoids and saponins that help regulate sleep patterns and reduce stress related to jet lag.
Flowers: Rich in hyperforin, a compound that helps adjust the body's natural circadian rhythms and promotes relaxation.
Seeds: Contain essential fatty acids and antioxidants that support adrenal function and reduce symptoms of fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh st. john's wort flowers at dawn on a sunny day for optimal potency.
Chop the st. john's wort flowers into small pieces and measure out 100 grams using a digital scale.
Combine the chopped flowers with 500 milliliters of vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring all plant material is submerged.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding solids and labeling with date and ingredients.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn tinctures helps with jet lag because of its adaptogenic properties, which allow it to gently adjust the body's natural rhythms. When taken at the right times, hawthorn tincture can help regulate sleep patterns and ease the symptoms of jet lag, such as fatigue, insomnia, and restlessness. Its calming effects also promote relaxation and reduce stress levels, making it easier to fall asleep on a new schedule. By supporting the body's natural circadian rhythms, hawthorn tincture helps to minimize the effects of jet lag and get you feeling refreshed and ready for your trip.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Hawthorn helps your body adapt to changes in time zones by reducing stress and promoting relaxation, which can alleviate symptoms of jet lag like fatigue and insomnia.

Cardiovascular benefits: Hawthorn's ability to improve blood flow and reduce blood pressure can help alleviate symptoms of dehydration and fatigue that often accompany jet lag, while also promoting overall cardiovascular health.

Antioxidant properties: Hawthorn's antioxidants help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by travel and time changes, which can contribute to inflammation and fatigue; they also support liver function and promote healthy detoxification processes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hawthorn roots contain bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to calm nervous system and promote relaxation, making them effective in helping alleviate symptoms of jet lag.
Barks: Hawthorn barks are rich in flavonoids and saponins, which can help regulate circadian rhythms and improve sleep quality, reducing the effects of jet lag.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality crataegus monogyna leaves and stems in their fresh state for optimal potency.
Cleanly chop the plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine one part chopped plant material with two parts 80-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking daily, to allow for maceration.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discard solids, and label with preparation date dosage instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with jet lag because its calming properties can soothe a restless body and mind, allowing you to adjust to new time zones more easily. The herb's natural sedative effects can promote relaxation and reduce stress levels, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep during your destination's local bedtime. Additionally, chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties may help alleviate symptoms of fatigue and irritability that often accompany jet lag.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) properties: Chamomile tincture helps to calm nervousness and anxiety associated with jet lag, making it easier to fall asleep and relax.
Sedative effects: Chamomile's sedative properties help to reduce restlessness, irritability, and insomnia caused by disrupted sleep patterns due to jet lag.

Anti-inflammatory and soothing effects: Chamomile tincture can also help to reduce inflammation and promote relaxation in the digestive system, which can alleviate symptoms of jet lag such as stomach upset and bloating.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin, a flavonoid that helps regulate sleep patterns and reduce anxiety associated with jet lag.
Flowers: High in azulene, a compound that promotes relaxation, reduces stress, and helps the body adjust to new time zones.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in sufficient quantity for the desired batch size.
Measure and combine one part of dried flowers with two parts of 95% ethanol or vodka.
Store the mixture in a dark glass jar at room temperature for eight weeks.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean dark glass bottle.
Label and store the finished tincture in a cool dark place for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Figwort tinctures helps with jet lag because it contains a unique blend of herbs that work in harmony to regulate the body's natural rhythms. The figwort plant has been used for centuries to treat various health issues, including fatigue and insomnia, which are common symptoms of jet lag. The tincture helps to calm the nervous system, promoting relaxation and reducing stress levels. Additionally, it can help to regulate the body's internal clock, making it easier to adjust to a new time zone.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Figwort's adaptogenic properties allow it to help your body adjust to new time zones by reducing stress and fatigue, making it easier to acclimate to a new sleep-wake cycle.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants in figwort tincture can help neutralize free radicals caused by jet lag-induced oxidative stress, which can contribute to symptoms like fatigue, headaches, and digestive issues.

Stress-relieving and calming effects: Figwort's sedative and anxiolytic properties can promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep despite the disruption of your regular sleep schedule.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the figwort plant contain a high concentration of alkaloids, which help to regulate the body's internal clock and reduce symptoms of jet lag.
Barks: The barks of the figwort plant have been traditionally used to treat circadian rhythm disorders, making them effective for alleviating jet lag symptoms such as insomnia and fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh scrophularia nodosa roots and dry them properly for at least seven days.
Chop the dried scrophularia nodosa roots into small pieces weighing exactly one kilogram each.
Combine the chopped roots with 95% ethanol in a ratio of 1:5 by weight and stir well for ten minutes.
Seal the mixture in dark glass bottles and store them at room temperature for six weeks, shaking daily for five minutes.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into clean dark glass bottles and discard the solids after allowing it to settle for two hours.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat jet lag with herbal tinctures is to combine adaptogenic herbs like Ashwagandha and Ginseng with calming botanicals such as Lavender and Valerian Root. These blends help regulate the body's circadian rhythms, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote relaxation. Take 20-30 drops of the tincture mixture one to two hours before bedtime, or as needed throughout the day, to ease the transition to a new time zone.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jet lag that are treated with herbal tinctures are those caused by time zone changes, fatigue, and disruptions to the body's natural rhythms. Herbal tinctures used for these ailments include Valerian root to promote relaxation and reduce stress, Ginkgo biloba to improve circulation and mental clarity, and Ashwagandha to boost energy and adaptability. These natural remedies can help alleviate symptoms of jet lag and other circadian rhythm disorders.
